overall_conclusion: Benchmark v3 is ready for presentation as a reproducible automated benchmark framework with clear claim boundaries. Layers 1–4 and Layer 6 are computed, while Layer 5 is reported as a quota-limited partial semantic audit. The benchmark supports artifact-level validation and controlled retrieval evaluation, but not full semantic-quality validation or full T001–T020 comparative superiority claims.

layer5_audit_details.claim_boundary: not full semantic coverage; no representative agent-level semantic estimate

baseline_support_matrix.claim_boundary: Baseline comparison is limited to the common-support subset. T004-T020 remain artifact-level validation unless baseline parity is proven. The benchmark does not support full T001-T020 comparative superiority claims.

layer5a_representativeness.interpretation: Layer 5A is not a representative semantic-quality estimate. Layer 5A is a quota-limited partial implementation audit.

layer5b_deterministic_proxy.claim_boundary: Layer 5B deterministic proxy supplements the quota-limited LLM judge audit. It does not replace LLM or human semantic evaluation and must not be reported as full semantic-quality validation.
